sections.paragraph:
ἀπάγχω (ἄγχω ‘to squeeze, strangle’) 1 aor. mid. ἀπηγξάμην (Hom.+; Jos., Ant. 12, 256; 15, 176) mid. hang oneself (so since Aeschyl., Hdt.; Epict.; PSI 177, 10; PGM 4, 1911; 2 Km 17:23; Tob 3:10.—Mlt. 155) of Judas Mt 27:5 (ἀπελθὼν ἀπήγξατο like Epict. 1, 2, 3). S. Ἰούδας 6.

aor. aor. = aorist

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

PSI PSI = Papiri greci e latini: Pubblicazioni della Società Italiana—List 4

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

Mlt. Mlt. = JMoulton; with page number alone specified, this abbr. refers to A Grammar of NT Greek I: Prolegomena—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 95
